hero_summary: Basta Nikar belongs to the Sikah family and, in concise Arabic explanation, is built from Jins Sikah on Iraq (B half-flat), followed by Jins Saba on Dukah (D).
short_identity: A Sikah-family maqam whose identity comes from moving between a Sikah color at the tonic on Iraq and a Saba color in the upper region on Dukah.

quick_facts.character: A dark and uncommon maqam with a Sikah foundation and a Saba upper region
quick_facts.common_start: It starts from the Sikah color on Iraq, then opens into Saba on Dukah before returning to the tonic

sayr.summary: Basta Nikar belongs clearly to the Sikah family. Comparative references place it among the maqamat built on Iraq (B half-flat) together with Iraq, Rahat al-Arwah, and Farahnak. In the concise Arabic explanation it consists of Jins Sikah on Iraq (B half-flat), followed by Jins Saba on Dukah (D). Analytical references also describe it as a model sequence of Sikah + Saba, which is the essential core of the maqam.
resting_tones: Iraq (B half-flat): the tonic and final resting point
Dukah (D): the crucial upper resting tone because it carries Jins Saba
Its Sikah color remains present below, but the maqam’s special identity emerges only when Saba on Dukah becomes audible above
motion_notes: Its essence is precisely the sequence Sikah then Saba
It belongs to the maqamat that do not produce a simple octave equivalence, largely because Saba is part of its internal structure
It should not be reduced to a fixed scale; rather, it is understood as a movement from a Sikah color on Iraq to Saba on Dukah and then back to the tonic
If Iraq is not firmly established, the maqam loses its Sikah-family anchor; if Saba on Dukah is not clear, it collapses into another Sikah-family maqam
common_path: Stabilize the tonic on Iraq (B half-flat)
Move convincingly into Saba on Dukah (D)
Return to Iraq with a clear closing cadence

common_modulations: Iraq is also built on Iraq (B half-flat) inside the Sikah family, but it is not defined by Saba on Dukah as Basta Nikar is; the main difference lies in the upper region and the sayr

Huzam belongs to the same family as well, but usually centers on Sikah (E half-flat) rather than Iraq (B half-flat), and its common melodic logic differs clearly from the Sikah + Saba sequence of Basta Nikar
Saba is present inside Basta Nikar as the second jins, but the maqam as a whole is not Saba, because its tonic, family identity, and lower structure remain Sikah-based on Iraq
